executiveSummary:
**Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis - January 2026**

National Medicaid enrollment has reached 75.3 million beneficiaries as of January 2026, with most major states experiencing enrollment declines following the end of pandemic-era continuous coverage protections. California leads with 12.6 million enrollees despite a significant 6.2% year-over-year decrease, while Texas maintains 4.0 million enrollees with a more moderate 3.1% decline. Notably, North Carolina bucked the national trend with a 0.5% enrollment increase, suggesting effective program management during the unwinding period. Total program expenditures reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, with a national per-enrollee cost of $2,869. Texas demonstrates relatively efficient spending at $2,988 per enrollee—4% above the national average but substantially lower than high-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,377) and Massachusetts ($4,201).

Quality performance remains highly variable across states, with a national average score of 65.6 out of 100 points. While ten states achieved high-performer status (≥75 points), fifteen states scored below 60, indicating persistent disparities in care delivery. Texas ranks 30th nationally with a moderate quality score of 62, trailing significantly behind top performers like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80) but outpacing lower-performing southeastern states. The state's managed care penetration of 93.3% substantially exceeds the national average of 83.2%, covering nearly 3.9 million beneficiaries. Recent federal regulatory activity focuses on HCBS quality measures for 2028 and enhanced interoperability standards, while ongoing OIG oversight suggests continued scrutiny of program integrity across all states. Leadership should prioritize quality improvement initiatives to advance Texas from moderate to high-performer status while maintaining the state's cost-efficiency advantage.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.03 million enrollees, representing a substantial but concerning position in the national landscape. The state's -3.1% year-over-year enrollment decline aligns closely with the national trend as pandemic-era continuous enrollment policies unwind, performing better than large states like California (-6.2%) and Illinois (-6.2%) but trailing North Carolina's +0.5% growth. At $2,988 per enrollee, Texas spends 4% above the national average of $2,869, positioning it in the middle tier among large states—significantly higher than Florida's lean $2,031 per enrollee but well below high-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,377) and Ohio ($3,561). This spending pattern reflects Texas's status as a non-expansion state, where higher per-capita costs often compensate for narrower eligibility criteria.

The state's managed care penetration of 93.3% represents a clear programmatic strength, exceeding the national average of 83.2% by 10 percentage points and demonstrating Texas's commitment to coordinated care delivery. However, the state's quality score of 62/100 ranks 30th nationally, placing it in the moderate performer category but below the national average of 65.6—a concerning gap given the state's size and managed care investment. This quality performance, while avoiding the bottom tier occupied by regional peers like Arkansas (54) and Oklahoma (51), suggests room for improvement in care coordination and outcomes. Looking ahead, Texas faces the dual challenge of maintaining program efficiency during enrollment normalization while addressing quality gaps, particularly as federal oversight intensifies around HCBS quality measures and interoperability standards that could impact the state's heavily managed care-dependent system.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 53% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,377 significantly exceeds the national average of $2,869, indicating potential cost efficiency concerns.

alerts.title: Florida Low Per-Enrollee Spending Raises Access Concerns
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,031 is 29% below the national average, potentially indicating inadequate service provision or access barriers.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critical Quality Performance
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi have quality scores below 55, indicating systemic care quality issues.

alerts.title: Massachusetts High Spending with Excellent Quality Outcomes
alerts.description: Massachusetts combines the highest quality score (81) with high per-enrollee spending ($4,201), demonstrating effective resource utilization.

alerts.title: Michigan Enrollment Decline Exceeds National Trend
alerts.description: Michigan's 7.6% year-over-year enrollment decline significantly exceeds the typical post-PHE unwinding patterns seen in other states.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid enrollment declining -3.1% YoY despite high MC penetration
executiveInsights.summary: Texas has 4.03M Medicaid enrollees, down 3.1% year-over-year, representing significant population loss. Despite this decline, Texas maintains 93.3% managed care penetration, well above the 83.2% national average.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment decline directly impacts contract value and scope while creating operational challenges for HHSC's managed care infrastructure that serves nearly all beneficiaries.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag enrollment trend analysis to HHSC leadership and assess contract volume implications

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs $2,988 vs national average $2,869 - efficiency gap
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $2,988 per Medicaid enrollee compared to the national average of $2,869, indicating 4.1% higher costs. Total Texas expenditures reach $12.05B annually.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-enrollee costs suggest potential efficiency opportunities that Accenture could address through process optimization or technology solutions.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Prepare cost efficiency analysis and benchmark against comparable large states

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score 62/100 ranks #30 nationally - improvement opportunity
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scores 62/100 on quality measures, ranking 30th out of 50 states in moderate tier. This is well below high performers like MA (81) and significantly above bottom performers like MS (48).
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Quality improvement represents a clear value proposition for Accenture services and aligns with HHSC priorities for better patient outcomes and federal compliance.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Develop quality improvement roadmap targeting specific measures where Texas underperforms

executiveInsights.title: New CMS HCBS quality measures for 2028 create compliance requirements
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows new Medicaid Home and Community-Based Services Quality Measure Set requirements for 2028. This represents upcoming regulatory changes affecting state programs.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Texas will need to implement new HCBS quality reporting capabilities, creating potential consulting and technology implementation opportunities for Accenture.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review HCBS quality measure requirements and assess Texas readiness gaps

executiveInsights.title: Texas procurement portal active - monitor competitive landscape
executiveInsights.summary: Texas HHSC Procurement Opportunities portal and ESBD systems show active procurement activities. Multiple procurement channels indicate ongoing contracting opportunities.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Active procurement signals potential new RFP opportunities or contract modifications that could expand Accenture's engagement scope with Texas HHSC.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Monitor Texas procurement portals weekly for relevant RFPs and contract amendments

riskOpportunity.label: Texas Medicaid MCO Recompete
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's 93.3% managed care penetration creates high-stakes recompetition opportunities for MCO contracts worth billions in annual expenditures.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor HHSC procurement portal and position for MCO support contract opportunities.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality Score Improvement Gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with 62/100 quality score, creating compliance risk but improvement opportunity.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop quality improvement consulting offerings targeting Texas's moderate performance tier.

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment Decline Impact
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.1% YoY, potentially reducing contract values and system utilization needs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Assess contract adjustment mechanisms and pivot toward efficiency-focused service offerings.

riskOpportunity.label: HCBS Quality Standards
riskOpportunity.description: New 2028 HCBS quality measures will require significant system updates and compliance infrastructure changes.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Build HCBS quality measurement capabilities and proactively engage Texas on compliance readiness.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG Audit Exposure
riskOpportunity.description: Active OIG oversight of Medicaid portfolios creates compliance risks for existing Texas contracts and operations.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Conduct proactive compliance review of all Texas HHSC deliverables and strengthen audit response protocols.

riskOpportunity.label: Interoperability Mandate Compliance
riskOpportunity.description: New interoperability and prior authorization standards require technical system overhauls across Medicaid infrastructure.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop interoperability consulting practice and engage Texas early on implementation planning.

riskOpportunity.label: Per-Enrollee Cost Efficiency
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's $2,988 per-enrollee cost exceeds national average, creating pressure for cost reduction initiatives.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position cost optimization and analytics capabilities to help Texas reduce per-enrollee expenditures.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive Displacement Risk
riskOpportunity.description: Multi-state competitors gaining ground in other large Medicaid markets could target Texas opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen Texas HHSC relationship management and differentiate through local partnership depth.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas Medicaid serves 4,032,864 enrollees as of January 2026.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $2,988 per Medicaid enrollee annually in fiscal year 2024.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 managed care enrollees.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas scored 62 out of 100 on CMS quality measures, ranking 30th of 50 states.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurement Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two Texas procurement portals are actively monitored for Medicaid opportunities.

pulseMetrics.label: OIG/Regulatory Signals
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Six active regulatory and oversight items tracked across OIG and Federal Register sources.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific milestone dates available in current data sources.
